explanations.1: This special line tells the system to use bash to run this script, even if it's called from a different shell.
explanations.3: The double colon in the function name is a naming convention to organize functions into a 'console' namespace, making it clear these are related utility functions.
explanations.6: This function name suggests it will prompt the user and wait for a yes or no answer, then return that choice.
explanations.12: This function is likely for printing messages only when verbose mode is enabled, so users can hide extra detail if they want.
explanations.18: A banner is typically a large decorative header printed to the screen, often used to display important information at the start of a script.
